name: Job Title Patterns — 师员家生人

cards.grammarNote: Chinese job titles are built from a small set of suffixes that signal what kind of role a person has. 老师, 工程师, 厨师 all end in 师. Once you recognize these suffixes, you can guess what an unfamiliar job title means.

师 = a skilled, often trained professional. Implies mastery of a craft or discipline. 老师 (teacher), 工程师 (engineer), 厨师 (chef), 律师 (lawyer), 医师 (physician), 设计师 (designer). If the job requires serious skill or training, it often takes 师.
老 (old/respected) + 师 (master) = teacher. 老 here is a respectful prefix, not about age. 老师 is also used as a direct address: 'Hello, teacher' = 老师好.
工程 (engineering/project) + 师 (skilled professional) = engineer. 工 = work, 程 = process/procedure.
厨 (kitchen) + 师 (skilled practitioner) = chef. 厨房 = kitchen (kitchen + room). 厨师 is specifically a professional cook.
律 (law / rule) + 师 = lawyer. 律法 = law/legal code.

cards.front: 员 — staff member / person in a role
cards.grammarNote: 员 = a person who fills a defined functional role, often in a service or organizational context. 服务员 (waiter/service staff), 演员 (actor), 售货员 (sales clerk), 运动员 (athlete), 营业员 (clerk). 员 implies a role within a system, not necessarily a specialized craft.
服务 (service) + 员 (person in role) = service person / waiter. 服务员！ is how you call a waiter in a restaurant — completely normal and not rude.
演 (to perform/act) + 员 = actor/performer. 演 also appears in 演讲 (speech/lecture) and 演出 (performance/show).
运动 (sports / exercise) + 员 = athlete. 运动 also means 'to exercise': 我喜欢运动 = I like sports.

cards.front: 家 — expert / master in a field
cards.grammarNote: 家 = an authority or creative master in a field. Often used for intellectual, artistic, or scholarly domains. 音乐家 (musician), 艺术家 (artist), 科学家 (scientist), 作家 (writer), 画家 (painter). 家 implies depth of expertise, not just a job role.
音乐 (music) + 家 (master/expert) = musician (in the serious, artistic sense). 音 = sound, 乐 = music/joy.
艺术 (art) + 家 = artist. 艺 = skill/art, 术 = technique/method.
作 (to write/make/do) + 家 = writer/author. 作家 specifically refers to literary or creative writers, not journalists (记者) or copywriters.

cards.front: 生 — person in a learning or medical role
cards.grammarNote: 生 = a person who is learning or practicing (especially medicine or study). 学生 (student), 医生 (doctor), 研究生 (graduate student), 实习生 (intern). 生 implies someone developing or applying knowledge — either as a learner or a practitioner.
医 (medicine/healing) + 生 (practitioner) = doctor. 医院 (hospital) shares the same 医. 大夫 (dàifu) is an alternative informal word for doctor.
学 (to learn/study) + 生 (person) = student. 学习 = to study (学 + 习 = learn + practice).

人 = person. Used to form nationalities and broad categories: 中国人 (Chinese person), 美国人 (American), 商人 (businessman), 工人 (worker), 本地人 (local person). 人 is the most general people-suffix — less about skill, more about identity or category.
商 (commerce/trade) + 人 = businessman. 商业 = business/commerce, 商店 = store/shop (商 + 店!).

cards.front: What's the difference: 音乐师, 音乐员, 音乐家?
cards.back: 师 = trained professional, 员 = role-filler, 家 = creative master
cards.grammarNote: 音乐师 = music teacher/instructor (trained skill). 音乐员 = a musician in an ensemble/band (role). 音乐家 = a musician of high artistic standing (master). The suffix shifts the nuance: skill → role → mastery.
